ddls:
CREATE TABLE "orders" (
  "order_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the order */,
  "order_date" TEXT /* Date when the order was placed */,
  "ship_date" TEXT /* Date when the order was shipped */,
  "ship_mode" TEXT /* Mode of shipping selected */,
  "customer_id" TEXT /* Identifier of the customer who made the order */,
  "segment" TEXT /* Customer segment classification */,
  "postal_code" TEXT /* Postal code of the customer */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the customer resides */,
  "state" TEXT /* State where the customer resides */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of the customer */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region classification */,
  "market" TEXT /* Market classification */,
  "priority" TEXT /* Priority level of the order */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the order */,
  "payment_method" TEXT /* Payment method used for the order */,
  "currency" TEXT /* Currency used for the transaction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("order_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_orders_segment FOREIGN KEY ("segment") REFERENCES segments ("segment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_orders_region FOREIGN KEY ("region") REFERENCES regions ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_orders_market FOREIGN KEY ("market") REFERENCES markets ("market_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_orders_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the customer */,
  "customer_name" TEXT /* Full name of the customer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  "address" TEXT /* Physical address of the customer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "products" (
  "product_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the product */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the product */,
  "sub_category" TEXT /* Sub-category of the product */,
  "product_name" TEXT /* Name of the product */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the product */,
  "price" REAL /* Price of the product */,
  "stock_level" INTEGER /* Current stock level of the product */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sales" (
  "order_id" TEXT /* Identifier for the order related to the sale */,
  "product_id" TEXT /* Identifier for the product being sold */,
  "sales" REAL /* Total sales amount for the transaction */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the product sold */,
  "discount" REAL /* Discount applied to the sale */,
  "profit" REAL /* Profit made from the sale */,
  "shipping_cost" REAL /* Shipping cost incurred for the sale */,
  "tax" REAL /* Tax amount applied to the sale */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("order_id", "product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "shipping" (
  "order_id" TEXT /* Identifier for the order being shipped */,
  "shipping_mode" TEXT /* Mode of shipping used for the order */,
  "shipping_cost" REAL /* Cost associated with shipping the order */,
  "shipping_date" TEXT /* Date when the order was shipped */,
  "estimated_delivery_date" TEXT /* Estimated date of delivery for the order */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("order_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "segments" (
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the customer segment */,
  "segment_name" TEXT /* Name of the customer segment */,
  "segment_description" TEXT /* Description of the customer segment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("segment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the geographical region */,
  "region_description" TEXT /* Description of the geographical region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "markets" (
  "market_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the market */,
  "market_name" TEXT /* Name of the market segment */,
  "market_description" TEXT /* Description of the market segment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("market_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "payments" (
  "payment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the payment */,
  "order_id" TEXT /* Identifier for the order related to the payment */,
  "payment_method" TEXT /* Method used for the payment */,
  "payment_date" TEXT /* Date when the payment was made */,
  "amount" REAL /* Amount paid */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("payment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_payments_order_id FOREIGN KEY ("order_id") REFERENCES orders ("order_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "order_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the order status update */,
  "order_id" TEXT /* Identifier for the order related to the status update */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the order */,
  "status_date" TEXT /* Date when the order status was updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_order_status_order_id FOREIGN KEY ("order_id") REFERENCES orders ("order_id")
)
